steps.title: Substitute a simpler variable
steps.content:
The expression $2x-7$ appears more than once, so let $u=2x-7$. This changes the equation into a simpler quadratic:
$$
u^2-8u+15=0
$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Factor the quadratic by finding two numbers that multiply to $15$ and add to $-8$. Those numbers are $-3$ and $-5$:
$$
u^2-8u+15=(u-3)(u-5)
$$
So $u$ must be $3$ or $5$.

steps.title: Back substitute for x
steps.content: Use $u=2x-7$ for each value. From $u=3$: $$ 2x-7=3 $$ $$ x=5 $$ From $u=5$: $$ 2x-7=5 $$ $$ x=6 $$ The list $5,6$ means either value works, not the number $56$. The answer is 5,6.
